🎨 Aesthetic Devising — Phase 1
Load an existing brief, generate a new one, approve, or delete. Run against a campaign slug.

Campaign Slug

Re-run Red Team

Identity Summary

Color Palette

#E5F3EC

#F2F7F4
textOnLight
#1E2B28
Hero Slogan
Sail first, spot the green.

Jewel of the Seas, EN/ES easy hellos.

Voice Persona
Your soft‑spoken plant friend who loves shaded windows and ocean horizons.

Casting Goal
A relaxed mix of plant‑curious travelers: solos, pairs, and small friend duos across ages. Most frames show one to three people in calm cruise moments, with a few family‑friendly scenes that still feel quiet and window‑side. Enforce representation via the casting matrix in Production Bible (see below).

Age Range Guidance
Core 25–60 with believable range: include a few teens (12–17) and several 60+ guests. No kiddie focus and no party‑only energy.

Diversity Intent
Visible diversity in skin tone, ethnicity (including Afro‑Caribbean and Latinx given San Juan embarkation), hair texture, and body type—across the full set, not token shots. Include queer representation and at least one visible mobility aid where feasible without shifting tone.

Pairing Guidance
Vary scene partners: solo at a rail, two friends of different backgrounds, a queer couple at a window, multigenerational duo in a lounge. Rotate who leads the moment; avoid repeating one default pair.

Anti-Stereotype Rules
• No “crazy plant person” tropes or gendered clichés
• No token bilingual ‘host’ as the only Latinx face
• No lab‑coat, expert, or instructor optics
• No leaf headpieces, faux vines, or cosplay
• Do not reduce culture to prints, flags, or palettes
Community Core Promise
Be around your plant community at sea—easy hellos, tiny compliments, and a calmer way to spot your people without turning your vacation into a schedule.

Participation Style
Drop in, drift out, or simply smile and pass by. Lurk‑friendly, low‑pressure, and brief by design—no tracking and no FOMO.

Social Gravity
A shared leaf print, a small enamel pin, or one EN/ES codename quietly signals common ground. Phone galleries with favorite leaf photos spark quick café‑nook chats once seated or at nearby benches—no device handling in active queues.

Copy Framing Rule
Use opt‑in language: “If you feel like it…,” “Swing by for 5–10 minutes,” “Lurk welcome.” Offer soft windows like around 08:15–08:45 and 17:15–17:45—or just say it when you pass by. Never imply reservations, rosters, or exclusive access. Privacy: share your own photos; avoid filming others.

Optional Gatherings
• Morning icon hello with one simple EN/ES hint—sometime around 08:15–08:45; in and out under 10 minutes
• Early‑evening ‘what you noticed’—around 17:15–17:45; two or three tiny shares, then dinner
• Sea‑day Camera‑Roll One‑Pick—pop‑by window of 10–12 minutes max; disperse immediately if a crowd forms
• Postcard Peek—bring one flat plant postcard to show‑only, or just listen
Red-Team Summary
Leaf & Leisure: Houseplant People Afloat is a well‑designed, ship‑first, ambient houseplant micro‑community concept with strong optionality, safety guardrails, bilingual inclusivity, and detailed production direction. However, there is a critical copy misalignment that re‑introduces precise anchor times and an incomplete rights/permissions plan for on‑ship capture that could jeopardize production. These must be fixed before approval.

block
Approval Recommendation
Do not approve yet. Resolve the precise‑time copy conflict everywhere and add an explicit ship‑level filming/permissions gate or commit to a synthetic/stock‑only capture plan before launch.

Required Fixes
• Remove all exact times (08:30/17:30) from the campaign metadata description and anywhere else; replace with non‑numerical phrasing and re‑QA all public copy against the preflight gate.
• Add an explicit ship/operator filming permissions policy and hard pre‑export block for any onboard capture without written approval; include documented fallback to synthetic/stock‑only deliverables if approvals are not obtained.
• Create and attach a casting coverage tracker mapped to each deliverable (video/still) with producer sign‑off to enforce the diversity matrix.
Issues
blocker
copy alignment
Precise times contradict optionality guardrails
Campaign description: “Recognition is an accessible icon+codename EN/ES (“MONSTERA / MONSTERA” + square) repeated at 08:30 and 17:30.” Production Bible gate says: “Block export if any precise time (e.g., 08:30/17:30) appears in copy.”

Purge exact times (08:30/17:30) from all materials, including the campaign metadata description. Public‑facing copy should use non‑numerical phrasing (e.g., “around breakfast” / “before dinner”) or broad windows without colons if absolutely needed. Re‑run a copy sweep to ensure zero precise times remain; update the revision note to reflect the fix.

blocker
production feasibility
Ship‑level capture permissions plan is incomplete
Permissions section covers venue naming/logos and allows handheld capture, but does not explicitly require ship/operator approval for commercial filming/photography. Line: “Any complex move is AI‑simulated only unless a written ship permit is secured” implies handheld could proceed without permission.

Add a rights/permissions checklist and a hard gate: “Block any onboard capture (handheld or otherwise) without written approval from the cruise line/ship for commercial use.” Provide a fallback path that commits deliverables to synthetic/stock only if approvals are not secured, and label such assets accordingly. Document port‑authority clearance or use S7_alt onboard fallback by default.

warning
diversity gap
Representation relies on intention; needs enforcement artifacts
Casting matrix is specified, but there’s no enforcement tracker tied to each deliverable (stills and video) to prove distribution across the final set.

Produce a casting coverage tracker with thumbnail placeholders mapped to every storyboard/still ID, indicating skin tone/ethnicity/age/queer visibility/mobility aid. Make Producer sign‑off a pre‑export gate to avoid tokenism or over‑repetition.

warning
optionality failure
Residual time windows risk schedule optics
Elevator pitch and community copy reference ranges like “around 08:15–08:45 or 17:15–17:45.” While softer, colons can still read like appointments.

For public copy, replace numeric ranges with colloquial anchors (e.g., “around breakfast” / “before dinner”). Keep any numeric guidance internal only. Ensure CTAs never imply appointment keeping.

warning
copy alignment
Merch line could imply event uniformity if over‑surfaced
Multiple CTAs include “Shop Leaf & Leisure tees”; merch videos exist alongside hero explainer.

Add a recurring line in merch placements: “Totally optional—no uniforms or identifiers required.” Keep merch frequency low in core campaign channels; avoid pairing merch shots with anchor mentions.

Generated: 3/14/2026, 9:04:30 PM
By: agent

    "globalDirectionNotes": "Vacation first, niche second. Keep the ship and sea leading every frame, with Leaf & Leisure expressed through behavior, spatial choice, or one tasteful cue. Do not feature venue signage or logos prominently. If approvals are later granted, match labels precisely.\nRelease/preflight checklist (blocker gates):\n- Block export if any branded venue name appears without linked written approval.\n- Block export if any shot note contradicts rail posture safety (forearms light, torso upright, hands inside rail line).\n- Block export if any edit depicts device handling in an active queue or filming in/near a line.\n- Block export if any precise time (e.g., 08:30/17:30) appears in copy.\nFallback asset list if permits denied: cabin/balcony/window angles (S4, S10 variants), promenade rails (S1, S7_alt_rail_return), library/card room window nooks, and licensed ocean/ship architecture stock without identifiable guests/crew. Maintain ship identity via rail/hull/window architecture; avoid exterior brand marks when licensing.\nSafety and privacy guardrails (must‑adhere):\n- No sitting/standing on rails; no leaning beyond the top rail; forearms resting lightly on rail, torso upright; hands remain inside rail line.\n- No device handling in active queues; recognition in/near lines is verbal‑only under 5 seconds; do not linger or film in/near a line—step to adjacent seating/benches for any share.\n- No filming while navigating stairs; keep clear of thresholds, service counters, and active queues.\n- No filming in or near gangways/security operations; port authority instructions override all.\n- Teens on‑camera only with guardian present and documented consent; guardians visible on set for any take.\n- Do not capture identifiable bystanders or crew without written consent; stage frames with clean backgrounds; blur/remove any incidental faces.\n- Phones shown waist‑high, angled down and away from others; no PII (boarding passes, cabin numbers, receipts) in frame.\n- No drones onboard or in port areas.\nCapture constraints: handheld or compact gimbal only. No cranes, dollies, steadicams, or tripods onboard. Any complex move is AI‑simulated only unless a written ship permit is secured.\nQueue etiquette: any recognition in or near a line is verbal‑only under 5 seconds; no filming; step to adjacent seating for any photo sharing.\nCasting matrix (enforce across shoots; ensure Afro‑Caribbean/Latinx presence in hero units and bilingual delivery):\n- Hero Explainer (video): On‑cam b‑roll only; VO delivered bilingually by Talent A (Afro‑Caribbean woman, 30s) and Talent B (Latinx man, 40s) alternating lines.\n- TikTok Seed: Visible pair includes one Afro‑Caribbean or Afro‑Latina woman and one Asian‑Caribbean or White Latinx non‑male; VO by Talent B (EN) with Talent A (ES tag).\n- Threshold Announcement (VO): Talent A (ES/EN) primary; caption bilingual.\n- Countdown 3‑2‑1: rotate VO among Talent B (EN/ES), Talent C (queer non‑binary Latinx, 20s), Talent D (White Puerto Rican man, 50s).\n- Stills LS1 (hero): Afro‑Caribbean woman + White man (30s–40s).\n- LS2 (hero_alt): Latinx woman + East Asian man (20s–30s).\n- LS3 (concept): Grandparent (Afro‑Caribbean, 60+) + teen (Latinx, 13–16; guardian present).\n- LS4 (email_header): Solo 60+ woman, mid/plus body type.\n- LS5 (social_square): Two women; one uses a slim cane visible at chair side.\n- LS6 (concept, adjacent to queue): Two strangers of different backgrounds (one Latinx, one White/Asian), no filming in line; if captured, staged from adjacent bench.\nQA: Run all EN/ES lines with Puerto Rican/Caribbean talent for naturalness and punctuation; rotate micro‑phrases lightly to avoid chantiness.\nAdvertising compliance: Avoid quantified scarcity. Use “Availability changes—check current options.”\nAlt‑asset labeling: Mark any shot that cannot be captured practically as “synthetic/AI‑only” in internal shot lists and exports.\nAnchor micro‑venue and crowd protocol: pre‑choose micro‑nooks in cafés/libraries away from thresholds; never block service counters, queues, muster paths, or egress lines. 🧪 Test Lab
Interactive Agent Dev
🎨 Aesthetic Devising — Phase 1
Load an existing brief, generate a new one, approve, or delete. Run against a campaign slug.

Campaign Slug
houseplant-botanical-caribbean-2026

Identity Summary

red-team pending
Color Palette
primary
#6FAF8F
secondary
#2D6860
accent
#E5F3EC
background
#F6F7F5
textOnDark
#F2F7F4
textOnLight
#1E2B28
Hero Slogan
Sail first, spot the green.

Jewel of the Seas, EN/ES easy hellos.

Voice Persona
Your soft‑spoken plant friend who loves shaded windows and ocean horizons.

Casting Goal
A relaxed mix of plant‑curious travelers: solos, pairs, and small friend duos across ages. Most frames show one to three people in calm cruise moments, with a few family‑friendly scenes that still feel quiet and window‑side. Enforce representation via the casting matrix in Production Bible (see below).

Age Range Guidance
Core 25–60 with believable range: include a few teens (12–17) and several 60+ guests. No kiddie focus and no party‑only energy.

Diversity Intent
Visible diversity in skin tone, ethnicity (including Afro‑Caribbean and Latinx given San Juan embarkation), hair texture, and body type—across the full set, not token shots. Include queer representation and at least one visible mobility aid where feasible without shifting tone.

Pairing Guidance
Vary scene partners: solo at a rail, two friends of different backgrounds, a queer couple at a window, multigenerational duo in a lounge. Rotate who leads the moment; avoid repeating one default pair.

Anti-Stereotype Rules
• No “crazy plant person” tropes or gendered clichés
• No token bilingual ‘host’ as the only Latinx face
• No lab‑coat, expert, or instructor optics
• No leaf headpieces, faux vines, or cosplay
• Do not reduce culture to prints, flags, or palettes
Community Core Promise
Be around your plant community at sea—easy hellos, tiny compliments, and a calmer way to spot your people without turning your vacation into a schedule.

Participation Style
Drop in, drift out, or simply smile and pass by. Lurk‑friendly, low‑pressure, and brief by design—no tracking and no FOMO.

Social Gravity
A shared leaf print, a small enamel pin, or one EN/ES codename quietly signals common ground. Phone galleries with favorite leaf photos spark quick café‑nook chats once seated or at nearby benches—no device handling in active queues.

Copy Framing Rule
Use opt‑in language: “If you feel like it…,” “Swing by for 5–10 minutes,” “Lurk welcome.” Offer soft windows like around breakfast and before dinner—or just say it when you pass by. Never imply reservations, rosters, or exclusive access. Privacy: share your own photos; avoid filming others.

Optional Gatherings
• Morning icon hello with one simple EN/ES hint—sometime in the breakfast window; in and out under 10 minutes
• Early‑evening ‘what you noticed’—before dinner; two or three tiny shares, then dinner
• Sea‑day Camera‑Roll One‑Pick—pop‑by window of 10–12 minutes max; disperse immediately if a crowd forms
• Postcard Peek—bring one flat plant postcard to show‑only, or just listen
Generated: 3/14/2026, 9:04:30 PM
By: agent

    "imageryMood": "Calm ocean-first travel with shaded window light, teak-and-glass textures, and small botanical hints. Feels like a breezy sea day or sail‑away pause—gentle, reflective, not staged.",
    "lightingStyle": "Caribbean golden hour and clear mid‑morning shade; soft window glow in cafés and libraries; light salt haze at rail. Avoid hard flash or dramatic spotlights.",
    "compositionNotes": "Lead with ship, ocean, or window view; add one subtle plant cue (a leaf‑print collar, an enamel pin, or a brief phone image). Favor negative space, horizon lines, and frame‑within‑frame windows. Keep tables clear; no spreads or signage.       "castingGoal": "A relaxed mix of plant‑curious travelers: solos, pairs, and small friend duos across ages. Enforce representation via the casting matrix in Production Bible (see below).",
      "ageRangeGuidance": "Core 25–60 with believable range: include a few teens (12–17) and several 60+ guests. No kiddie focus and no party‑only energy.",
      "diversityIntent": "Visible diversity in skin tone, ethnicity (including Afro‑Caribbean and Latinx given San Juan embarkation), hair texture, and body type—across the full set, not token shots.       "pairingGuidance": "Vary scene partners: solo at a rail, two friends of different backgrounds, a queer couple at a window, multigenerational duo in a lounge. Rotate who leads the moment; avoid repeating one default pair.",
      "stylingGuidance": "Resort‑casual linens and cottons in soft greens, sands, and navies; one subtle botanical print per frame max. Natural grooming, minimal accessories, comfortable sandals/sneakers. No costumes, aprons, gardening gloves, or prop‑heavy looks.",

    "globalDirectionNotes": "Vacation first, niche second. Keep the ship and sea leading every frame, with Leaf & Leisure expressed through behavior, spatial choice, or one tasteful cue. Never stage a class or demo. Avoid any non‑native greenery. Maintain small social scale—mostly pairs, occasional serene solo—so the community feels easy and opt‑in. Rotate cues: behavioral nods, shaded seat choice, architectural motifs, one postcard cameo total across a set, one sketchbook, one scarf. Use the single poetic leaf‑shadow only once (S6). Keep storyboards environment‑led so downstream models don’t need human animation; let wake, flags, reflections, and clouds provide motion.\nPermissions, rights, and naming:\n- Ship/operator approvals: Block any onboard capture (handheld or otherwise) without written approval from the cruise line/ship for commercial use. If approvals are not secured, commit deliverables to synthetic/stock‑only assets and label accordingly in exports.\n- Port/terminal approvals: Do not film on piers or terminals without written port/authority clearance. If denied or impractical, default to S7_alt_rail_return.\n- Venue naming/logos: Until written venue approvals are on file, all public‑facing labels remain generic (e.g., “café window seats,” “buffet window,” “atrium,” “forward‑view lounge”). Do not feature venue signage or logos prominently. If approvals are later granted, match labels precisely.\nRelease/preflight checklist (blocker gates):\n- Block export if any branded venue name appears without linked written approval.\n- Block export if any precise time (e.g., 08:30/17:30) appears in public copy; use colloquial anchors (“around breakfast,” “before dinner”).\n- Block export for any onboard footage lacking written ship/operator filming approval.\n- Block export for any pier/terminal footage lacking written port/authority approval.\n- Block export if any shot note contradicts rail posture safety (forearms light, torso upright, hands inside rail line).\n- Block export if any edit depicts device handling in an active queue or filming in/near a line.\n- Producer must upload a Casting Coverage Tracker mapping every storyboard shot and still (IDs) to on‑camera identities: skin tone/ethnicity, age band, queer visibility, and any mobility aid. Sign‑off required to ensure matrix coverage; block export until complete.\nFallback asset plan: cabin/balcony/window angles (S4, S10 variants), promenade rails (S1, S7_alt_rail_return), library/card room window nooks, and licensed ocean/ship architecture stock without identifiable guests/crew. Maintain ship identity via rail/hull/window architecture; avoid exterior brand marks when licensing. If approvals not secured, deliver synthetic/stock‑only versions and label assets “synthetic/AI‑only” in internal shot lists and exports.\nSafety, privacy, and accessibility guardrails (must‑adhere):\n- No sitting/standing on rails; no leaning beyond the top rail; forearms resting lightly on rail, torso upright; hands remain inside rail line.\n- No device handling in active queues; recognition in/near lines is verbal‑only under 5 seconds; do not linger or film in/near a line—step to adjacent seating/benches for any share.\n- No filming while navigating stairs; keep clear of thresholds, service counters, and active queues.\n- No filming in or near gangways/security operations; port authority instructions override all.\n- Teens on‑camera only with guardian present and documented consent; guardians visible on set for any take.\n- Do not capture identifiable bystanders or crew without written consent; stage frames with clean backgrounds; blur/remove any incidental faces.\n- Phones shown waist‑high, angled down and away from others; no PII (boarding passes, cabin numbers, receipts) in frame.\n- Offer seated options and quiet corners for mobility‑aid users; avoid prolonged standing shots.\n- No drones onboard or in port areas.\nCapture constraints: handheld or compact gimbal only. No cranes, dollies, steadicams, or tripods onboard. Any complex move is AI‑simulated only unless a written ship permit is secured.\nQueue etiquette: any recognition in or near a line is verbal‑only under 5 seconds; no filming; step to adjacent seating for any photo sharing.\nCasting matrix (enforce across shoots; ensure Afro‑Caribbean/Latinx presence in hero units and bilingual delivery):\n- Hero Explainer (video): On‑cam b‑roll only; VO delivered bilingually by Talent A (Afro‑Caribbean woman, 30s) and Talent B (Latinx man, 40s) alternating lines.\n- TikTok Seed: Visible pair includes one Afro‑Caribbean or Afro‑Latina woman and one Asian‑Caribbean or White Latinx non‑male; VO by Talent B (EN) with Talent A (ES tag).\n- Threshold Announcement (VO): Talent A (ES/EN) primary; caption bilingual.\n- Countdown 3‑2‑1: rotate VO among Talent B (EN/ES), Talent C (queer non‑binary Latinx, 20s), Talent D (White Puerto Rican man, 50s).\n- Stills LS1 (hero): Afro‑Caribbean woman + White man (30s–40s).\n- LS2 (hero_alt): Latinx woman + East Asian man (20s–30s).\n- LS3 (concept): Grandparent (Afro‑Caribbean, 60+) + teen (Latinx, 13–16; guardian present).\n- LS4 (email_header): Solo 60+ woman, mid/plus body type.\n- LS5 (social_square): Two women; one uses a slim cane visible at chair side.\n- LS6 (concept, adjacent to queue): Two strangers of different backgrounds (one Latinx, one White/Asian), no filming in line; if captured, staged from adjacent bench.\nQA: Run all EN/ES lines with Puerto Rican/Caribbean talent for naturalness and punctuation; rotate micro‑phrases lightly to avoid chantiness. Include a bilingual copy QA checklist in the producer packet.\nAlt‑asset labeling: Mark any shot that cannot be captured practically as “synthetic/AI‑only” in internal shot lists and exports.\nAnchor micro‑venue and crowd protocol: pre‑choose and photo‑document 2–3 micro‑nooks per soft window (cafés/libraries) away from thresholds; never block service counters, queues, muster paths, or egress lines. Hard cap: max 6 participants visible at once; 10‑minute linger cap; if a crowd forms, hosts disperse and relocate or end the moment.",

🧪 Test Lab
Interactive Agent Dev
🎨 Aesthetic Devising — Phase 1
Load an existing brief, generate a new one, approve, or delete. Run against a campaign slug.

Campaign Slug

Identity Summary

Color Palette

textOnDark
#F2F7F4
textOnLight
#1E2B28
Hero Slogan
Sail first, spot the green.

Jewel of the Seas, EN/ES easy hellos.

Voice Persona
Your soft‑spoken plant friend who loves shaded windows and ocean horizons.

Casting Goal
A relaxed mix of plant‑curious travelers: solos, pairs, and small friend duos across ages. Most frames show one to three people in calm cruise moments, with a few family‑friendly scenes that still feel quiet and window‑side. Enforce representation via the casting matrix in Production Bible (see below).

Age Range Guidance
Core 25–60 with believable range: include a few teens (12–17) and several 60+ guests. No kiddie focus and no party‑only energy.

Diversity Intent
Visible diversity in skin tone, ethnicity (including Afro‑Caribbean and Latinx given San Juan embarkation), hair texture, and body type—across the full set, not token shots. Include queer representation and at least one visible mobility aid where feasible without shifting tone.

Pairing Guidance
Vary scene partners: solo at a rail, two friends of different backgrounds, a queer couple at a window, multigenerational duo in a lounge. Rotate who leads the moment; avoid repeating one default pair.

Anti-Stereotype Rules
• No “crazy plant person” tropes or gendered clichés
• No token bilingual ‘host’ as the only Latinx face
• No lab‑coat, expert, or instructor optics
• No leaf headpieces, faux vines, or cosplay
• Do not reduce culture to prints, flags, or palettes
Community Core Promise
Be around your plant community at sea—easy hellos, tiny compliments, and a calmer way to spot your people without turning your vacation into a schedule.

Participation Style
Drop in, drift out, or simply smile and pass by. Lurk‑friendly, low‑pressure, and brief by design—no tracking and no FOMO.

Social Gravity
A shared leaf print, a small enamel pin, or one EN/ES codename quietly signals common ground. Phone galleries with favorite leaf photos spark quick café‑nook chats once seated or at nearby benches—no device handling in active queues.

Copy Framing Rule
Use opt‑in language: “If you feel like it…,” “Swing by for 5–10 minutes,” “Lurk welcome.” Offer soft windows like around breakfast and before dinner—or just say it when you pass by. Never imply reservations, rosters, or exclusive access. Privacy: share your own photos; avoid filming others.

Optional Gatherings
• Morning icon hello with one simple EN/ES hint—sometime in the breakfast window; in and out under 10 minutes
• Early‑evening ‘what you noticed’—before dinner; two or three tiny shares, then dinner
• Sea‑day Camera‑Roll One‑Pick—pop‑by window of 10–12 minutes max; disperse immediately if a crowd forms
• Postcard Peek—bring one flat plant postcard to show‑only, or just listen
Red-Team Summary
Thoughtful, ship‑first, ambient concept with excellent optionality, safety, permissions gating, and a strong diversity plan. However, a key copy conflict encourages phone sharing in a café queue, which violates the campaign’s own no‑devices‑in‑line guardrail; the public‑facing metadata still mentions precise anchor times (08:30/17:30), contradicting the new rule to use colloquial anchors; and brand/ship naming lacks explicit trademark/disclaimer language. These issues must be resolved before launch.

block
Approval Recommendation
Block launch until the queue‑handling copy is corrected everywhere, all precise times are purged from metadata and any generators, and ship/brand naming rights and disclaimers are added and gated in pre‑export checks.

Required Fixes
• Rewrite the ‘Café queue chats while showing one camera‑roll favorite’ moment to comply with no‑devices‑in‑line; sweep and correct all instances across campaign docs and social; add a pre‑export keyword gate to prevent reintroduction.
• Remove all precise anchor times (e.g., 08:30/17:30) from metadata and any generation seeds; enforce with a regex‑based pre‑export block for time strings in public outputs.
• Add ship/brand naming permissions and a standard trademark/disclaimer line to all placements that reference Jewel of the Seas/Royal Caribbean; add a pre‑export check to verify presence.
Issues
blocker
copy alignment
Queue device‑handling conflict encourages behavior banned elsewhere
Top‑level cruiseNativeMoments includes: “Café queue chats while showing one camera‑roll favorite, then dispersing.” Production Bible and community rules say: “No device handling in active queues; recognition in/near lines is verbal‑only; step to adjacent seating for any share.”

Replace the cruiseNativeMoments line with a queue‑safe variant (e.g., “Café approach hello, then once seated nearby show one camera‑roll favorite”) and sweep the entire campaign (all docs, social, and storyboards) to remove any suggestion of showing phones while in a line. Add a pre‑export keyword gate blocking ‘queue + photo/phone/show’ collisions.

warning
copy alignment
Precise anchor times still present in metadata
Campaign metadata description: “icon+codename … repeated at 08:30 and 17:30,” while Revision Notes claim all precise times were purged from public copy and the Production Bible blocks precise times.

Purge 08:30/17:30 from the metadata and any internal seeds/generators. Lock a pre‑export regex gate for time patterns (e.g., \b([01]?\d|2[0-3]):[0-5]\d\b) across all public‑facing outputs and metadata.

warning
production feasibility
Ship/brand naming lacks explicit rights and disclaimer handling
Marketing copy names Jewel of the Seas and Royal Caribbean without an explicit trademark/disclaimer line or confirmation of brand‑use permissions beyond filming approvals.

Confirm contractual permission to name ship/operator in marketing. Add a standard footer: “Jewel of the Seas and Royal Caribbean International are trademarks of their respective owners and are not sponsors of nor responsible for this program.” Add a pre‑export check that a trademark/disclaimer line is present on landing pages, emails, and paid ads referencing the ship/brand.

    "globalDirectionNotes": "Vacation first, niche second. Keep the ship and sea leading every frame, with Leaf & Leisure expressed through behavior, spatial choice, or one tasteful cue. Never stage a class or demo. Avoid any non‑native greenery. Maintain small social scale—mostly pairs, occasional serene solo—so the community feels easy and opt‑in. Rotate cues: behavioral nods, shaded seat choice, architectural motifs, one postcard cameo total across a set, one sketchbook, one scarf. Use the single poetic leaf‑shadow only once (S6). Keep storyboards environment‑led so downstream models don’t need human animation; let wake, flags, reflections, and clouds provide motion.\nPermissions, rights, and naming:\n- Ship/operator approvals: Block any onboard capture (handheld or otherwise) without written approval from the cruise line/ship for commercial use. If approvals are not secured, commit deliverables to synthetic/stock‑only assets and label accordingly in exports.\n- Port/terminal approvals: Do not film on piers or terminals without written port/authority clearance. If denied or impractical, default to S7_alt_rail_return.\n- Venue naming/logos: Until written venue approvals are on file, all public‑facing labels remain generic (e.g., “café window seats,” “buffet window,” “atrium,” “forward‑view lounge”). Do not feature venue signage or logos prominently. If approvals are later granted, match labels precisely.\nRelease/preflight checklist (blocker gates):\n- Block export if any branded venue name appears without linked written approval.\n- Block export if any precise time (e.g., 08:30/17:30) appears in public copy; use colloquial anchors (“around breakfast,” “before dinner”).\n- Block export for any onboard footage lacking written ship/operator filming approval.\n- Block export for any pier/terminal footage lacking written port/authority approval.\n- Block export if any shot note contradicts rail posture safety (forearms light, torso upright, hands inside rail line).\n- Block export if any edit depicts device handling in an active queue or filming in/near a line.\n- Producer must upload a Casting Coverage Tracker mapping every storyboard shot and still (IDs) to on‑camera identities: skin tone/ethnicity, age band, queer visibility, and any mobility aid. Sign‑off required to ensure matrix coverage; block export until complete.\nFallback asset plan: cabin/balcony/window angles (S4, S10 variants), promenade rails (S1, S7_alt_rail_return), library/card room window nooks, and licensed ocean/ship architecture stock without identifiable guests/crew. Maintain ship identity via rail/hull/window architecture; avoid exterior brand marks when licensing. If approvals not secured, deliver synthetic/stock‑only versions and label assets “synthetic/AI‑only” in internal shot lists and exports.\nSafety, privacy, and accessibility guardrails (must‑adhere):\n- No sitting/standing on rails; no leaning beyond the top rail; forearms resting lightly on rail, torso upright; hands remain inside rail line.\n- No device handling in active queues; recognition in/near lines is verbal‑only under 5 seconds; do not linger or film in/near a line—step to adjacent seating/benches for any share.\n- No filming while navigating stairs; keep clear of thresholds, service counters, and active queues.\n- No filming in or near gangways/security operations; port authority instructions override all.\n- Teens on‑camera only with guardian present and documented consent; guardians visible on set for any take.\n- Do not capture identifiable bystanders or crew without written consent; stage frames with clean backgrounds; blur/remove any incidental faces.\n- Phones shown waist‑high, angled down and away from others; no PII (boarding passes, cabin numbers, receipts) in frame.\n- Offer seated options and quiet corners for mobility‑aid users; avoid prolonged standing shots.\n- No drones onboard or in port areas.\nCapture constraints: handheld or compact gimbal only. No cranes, dollies, steadicams, or tripods onboard. Any complex move is AI‑simulated only unless a written ship permit is secured.\nQueue etiquette: any recognition in or near a line is verbal‑only under 5 seconds; no filming; step to adjacent seating for any photo sharing.\nCasting matrix (enforce across shoots; ensure Afro‑Caribbean/Latinx presence in hero units and bilingual delivery):\n- Hero Explainer (video): On‑cam b‑roll only; VO delivered bilingually by Talent A (Afro‑Caribbean woman, 30s) and Talent B (Latinx man, 40s) alternating lines.\n- TikTok Seed: Visible pair includes one Afro‑Caribbean or Afro‑Latina woman and one Asian‑Caribbean or White Latinx non‑male; VO by Talent B (EN) with Talent A (ES tag).\n- Threshold Announcement (VO): Talent A (ES/EN) primary; caption bilingual.\n- Countdown 3‑2‑1: rotate VO among Talent B (EN/ES), Talent C (queer non‑binary Latinx, 20s), Talent D (White Puerto Rican man, 50s).\n- Stills LS1 (hero): Afro‑Caribbean woman + White man (30s–40s).\n- LS2 (hero_alt): Latinx woman + East Asian man (20s–30s).\n- LS3 (concept): Grandparent (Afro‑Caribbean, 60+) + teen (Latinx, 13–16; guardian present).\n- LS4 (email_header): Solo 60+ woman, mid/plus body type.\n- LS5 (social_square): Two women; one uses a slim cane visible at chair side.\n- LS6 (concept, adjacent to queue): Two strangers of different backgrounds (one Latinx, one White/Asian), no filming in line; if captured, staged from adjacent bench.\nQA: Run all EN/ES lines with Puerto Rican/Caribbean talent for naturalness and punctuation; rotate micro‑phrases lightly to avoid chantiness. Include a bilingual copy QA checklist in the producer packet.\nAlt‑asset labeling: Mark any shot that cannot be captured practically as “synthetic/AI‑only” in internal shot lists and exports.\nAnchor micro‑venue and crowd protocol: pre‑choose and photo‑document 2–3 micro‑nooks per soft window (cafés/libraries) away from thresholds; never block service counters, queues, muster paths, or egress lines. Hard cap: max 6 participants visible at once; 10‑minute linger cap; if a crowd forms, hosts disperse and relocate or end the moment.",

        "referenceCategory": "dining"
      }
    ],
    "globalDirectionNotes": "Every still reads first as a dream vacation moment on the ship—ocean, rails, atrium light—then quietly signals Leaf & Leisure through behavior, spatial choice, or one subtle cue. Keep surfaces clear, avoid staged spreads, and favor window benches, rails, and airy negative space for copy. Maintain casting variety across ages and backgrounds; show relaxed pairs in most frames with one serene solo. No lanyards depicted in marketing stills; recognition cues are wardrobe or verbal only in public visuals. Choose micro‑nooks away from thresholds; never block service counters or egress. Do not shoot in active queues; any recognition there is verbal‑only and not filmed. One postcard cameo total per set; then tuck away.   "redTeamReview": {
    "verdict": "block",
    "summary": "Thoughtful, ship‑first, ambient concept with excellent optionality, safety, permissions gating, and a strong diversity plan. However, a key copy conflict encourages phone sharing in a café queue, which violates the campaign’s own no‑devices‑in‑line guardrail; the public‑facing metadata still mentions precise anchor times (08:30/17:30), contradicting the new rule to use colloquial anchors; and brand/ship naming lacks explicit trademark/disclaimer language. These issues must be resolved before launch.",
    "approvalRecommendation": "Block launch until the queue‑handling copy is corrected everywhere, all precise times are purged from metadata and any generators, and ship/brand naming rights and disclaimers are added and gated in pre‑export checks.",
    "strengths": [
      "Ambient, opt‑in rhythm with micro hellos and tiny compliments; no workshop or event‑takeover drift",
      "Cruise‑native scenes, clear rail‑safety and queue‑etiquette rules; solid privacy guidance",
      "Comprehensive production bible with approvals gates, synthetic/stock fallback, and pre‑export blockers",
      "Robust casting matrix with Afro‑Caribbean/Latinx presence, queer visibility, and a mobility aid included",
      "Visual and prop system is plausible onboard: ship/sea first, no live plants or swap optics, no signage clutter"
    ],
    "issues": [
      {
        "category": "copy_alignment",
        "severity": "blocker",
        "title": "Queue device‑handling conflict encourages behavior banned elsewhere",
        "evidence": "Top‑level cruiseNativeMoments includes: “Café queue chats while showing one camera‑roll favorite, then dispersing.” Production Bible and community rules say: “No device handling in active queues; recognition in/near lines is verbal‑only; step to adjacent seating for any share.”",
        "recommendation": "Replace the cruiseNativeMoments line with a queue‑safe variant (e.g., “Café approach hello, then once seated nearby show one camera‑roll favorite”) and sweep the entire campaign (all docs, social, and storyboards) to remove any suggestion of showing phones while in a line. Add a pre‑export keyword gate blocking ‘queue + photo/phone/show’ collisions."
      },
      {
        "category": "copy_alignment",
        "severity": "warning",
        "title": "Precise anchor times still present in metadata",

        "recommendation": "Purge 08:30/17:30 from the metadata and any internal seeds/generators. Lock a pre‑export regex gate for time patterns (e.g., \\b([01]?\\d|2[0-3]):[0-5]\\d\\b) across all public‑facing outputs and metadata."
      },
      {
        "category": "production_feasibility",

🧪 Test Lab
Interactive Agent Dev
🎨 Aesthetic Devising — Phase 1
Load an existing brief, generate a new one, approve, or delete. Run against a campaign slug.

Campaign Slug
houseplant-botanical-caribbean-2026

textOnLight
#1E2B28
Hero Slogan
Sail first, spot the green.

Jewel of the Seas, EN/ES easy hellos.

Voice Persona
Your soft‑spoken plant friend who loves shaded windows and ocean horizons.

Casting Goal
A relaxed mix of plant‑curious travelers: solos, pairs, and small friend duos across ages. Most frames show one to three people in calm cruise moments, with a few family‑friendly scenes that still feel quiet and window‑side. Enforce representation via the casting matrix in Production Bible (see below).

Age Range Guidance
Core 25–60 with believable range: include a few teens (12–17) and several 60+ guests. No kiddie focus and no party‑only energy.

Diversity Intent
Visible diversity in skin tone, ethnicity (including Afro‑Caribbean and Latinx given San Juan embarkation), hair texture, and body type—across the full set, not token shots. Include queer representation and at least one visible mobility aid where feasible without shifting tone.

Pairing Guidance
Vary scene partners: solo at a rail, two friends of different backgrounds, a queer couple at a window, multigenerational duo in a lounge. Rotate who leads the moment; avoid repeating one default pair.

Anti-Stereotype Rules
• No “crazy plant person” tropes or gendered clichés
• No token bilingual ‘host’ as the only Latinx face
• No lab‑coat, expert, or instructor optics
• No leaf headpieces, faux vines, or cosplay
• Do not reduce culture to prints, flags, or palettes
Community Core Promise
Be around your plant community at sea—easy hellos, tiny compliments, and a calmer way to spot your people without turning your vacation into a schedule.

Participation Style
Drop in, drift out, or simply smile and pass by. Lurk‑friendly, low‑pressure, and brief by design—no tracking and no FOMO.

Social Gravity
A shared leaf print, a small enamel pin, or one EN/ES codename quietly signals common ground. Phone galleries with favorite leaf photos spark quick café‑nook chats once seated or at nearby benches—no device handling in active queues.

Copy Framing Rule
Use opt‑in language: “If you feel like it…,” “Swing by for 5–10 minutes,” “Lurk welcome.” Offer soft windows like around breakfast and before dinner—or just say it when you pass by. Never imply reservations, rosters, or exclusive access. Privacy: share your own photos; avoid filming others.

Optional Gatherings
• Morning icon hello with one simple EN/ES hint—sometime in the breakfast window; in and out under 10 minutes
• Early‑evening ‘what you noticed’—before dinner; two or three tiny shares, then dinner
• Sea‑day Camera‑Roll One‑Pick—pop‑by window of 10–12 minutes max; disperse immediately if a crowd forms
• Postcard Peek—bring one flat plant postcard to show‑only, or just listen
Generated: 3/14/2026, 9:04:30 PM
By: agent

    "globalDirectionNotes": "Vacation first, niche second. Keep the ship and sea leading every frame, with Leaf & Leisure expressed through behavior, spatial choice, or one tasteful cue. Never stage a class or demo. Avoid any non‑native greenery. Maintain small social scale—mostly pairs, occasional serene solo—so the community feels easy and opt‑in. Rotate cues: behavioral nods, shaded seat choice, architectural motifs, one postcard cameo total across a set, one sketchbook, one scarf. Use the single poetic leaf‑shadow only once (S6). Keep storyboards environment‑led so downstream models don’t need human animation; let wake, flags, reflections, and clouds provide motion.\nPermissions, rights, and naming:\n- Ship/operator approvals: Block any onboard capture (handheld or otherwise) without written approval from the cruise line/ship for commercial use. If approvals are not secured, commit deliverables to synthetic/stock‑only assets and label accordingly in exports.\n- Port/terminal approvals: Do not film on piers or terminals without written port/authority clearance. If denied or impractical, default to S7_alt_rail_return.\n- Venue naming/logos: Until written venue approvals are on file, all public‑facing labels remain generic (e.g., “café window seats,” “buffet window,” “atrium,” “forward‑view lounge”). Do not feature venue signage or logos prominently. If approvals are later granted, match labels precisely.\n- Brand/trademark usage: Do not reference “Jewel of the Seas” or “Royal Caribbean International” in any public unit unless contractual permission to name the ship/operator is confirmed. Where referenced, include the exact disclaimer: “Jewel of the Seas and Royal Caribbean International are trademarks of their respective owners and are not sponsors of nor responsible for this program.”\nRelease/preflight checklist (blocker gates):\n- Block export if any branded venue name appears without linked written approval.\n- Block export if any precise time (e.g., 08:30/17:30) appears in public copy or metadata; use colloquial anchors (“around breakfast,” “before dinner”). Apply regex: /\\b([01]?\\d|2[0-3]):[0-5]\\d\\b/.\n- Block export for any onboard footage lacking written ship/operator filming approval.\n- Block export for any pier/terminal footage lacking written port/authority approval.\n- Block export if any shot note contradicts rail posture safety (forearms light, torso upright, hands inside rail line).\n- Block export if any edit depicts device handling in an active queue or filming in/near a line. Add keyword gate: /(queue|line).*(phone|photo|camera|show)/i.\n- Block export if any public unit references the ship/operator without the required trademark disclaimer present (landing pages, email footers, paid ads).\n- Producer must upload a Casting Coverage Tracker mapping every storyboard shot and still (IDs) to on‑camera identities: skin tone/ethnicity, age band, queer visibility, and any mobility aid. Sign‑off required to ensure matrix coverage; block export until complete.\nFallback asset plan: cabin/balcony/window angles (S4, S10 variants), promenade rails (S1, S7_alt_rail_return), library/card room window nooks, and licensed ocean/ship architecture stock without identifiable guests/crew. Maintain ship identity via rail/hull/window architecture; avoid exterior brand marks when licensing. If approvals not secured, deliver synthetic/stock‑only versions and label assets “synthetic/AI‑only” in internal shot lists and exports.\nSafety, privacy, and accessibility guardrails (must‑adhere):\n- No sitting/standing on rails; no leaning beyond the top rail; forearms resting lightly on rail, torso upright; hands remain inside rail line.\n- No device handling in active queues; recognition in/near lines is verbal‑only under 5 seconds; do not linger or film in/near a line—step to adjacent seating/benches for any share.\n- No filming while navigating stairs; keep clear of thresholds, service counters, and active queues.\n- No filming in or near gangways/security operations; port authority instructions override all.\n- Teens on‑camera only with guardian present and documented consent; guardians visible on set for any take.\n- Do not capture identifiable bystanders or crew without written consent; stage frames with clean backgrounds; blur/remove any incidental faces.\n- Phones shown waist‑high, angled down and away from others; no PII (boarding passes, cabin numbers, receipts) in frame.\n- Offer seated options and quiet corners for mobility‑aid users; avoid prolonged standing shots.\n- Include non‑visual participation cues in VO/copy: textures (teak/linen), breeze sounds, ambient ship hums—so low‑vision guests can enjoy the motif without visuals.\n- No drones onboard or in port areas.\nCapture constraints: handheld or compact gimbal only. No cranes, dollies, steadicams, or tripods onboard. Any complex move is AI‑simulated only unless a written ship permit is secured.\nQueue etiquette: any recognition in or near a line is verbal‑only under 5 seconds; no filming; step to adjacent seating for any photo sharing.\nCasting matrix (enforce across shoots; ensure Afro‑Caribbean/Latinx presence in hero units and bilingual delivery):\n- Hero Explainer (video): On‑cam b‑roll only; VO delivered bilingually by Talent A (Afro‑Caribbean woman, 30s) and Talent B (Latinx man, 40s) alternating lines.\n- TikTok Seed: Visible pair includes one Afro‑Caribbean or Afro‑Latina woman and one Asian‑Caribbean or White Latinx non‑male; VO by Talent B (EN) with Talent A (ES tag).\n- Threshold Announcement (VO): Talent A (ES/EN) primary; caption bilingual.\n- Countdown 3‑2‑1: rotate VO among Talent B (EN/ES), Talent C (queer non‑binary Latinx, 20s), Talent D (White Puerto Rican man, 50s).\n- Stills LS1 (hero): Afro‑Caribbean woman + White man (30s–40s).\n- LS2 (hero_alt): Latinx woman + East Asian man (20s–30s).\n- LS3 (concept): Grandparent (Afro‑Caribbean, 60+) + teen (Latinx, 13–16; guardian present).\n- LS4 (email_header): Solo 60+ woman, mid/plus body type.\n- LS5 (social_square): Two women; one uses a slim cane visible at chair side.\n- LS6 (concept, adjacent to queue): Two strangers of different backgrounds (one Latinx, one White/Asian), no filming in line; if captured, staged from adjacent bench.\nQA: Run all EN/ES lines with Puerto Rican/Caribbean talent for naturalness and punctuation; rotate micro‑phrases lightly to avoid chantiness. Include a bilingual copy QA checklist in the producer packet.\nAlt‑asset labeling: Mark any shot that cannot be captured practically as “synthetic/AI‑only” in internal shot lists and exports.\nAnchor micro‑venue and crowd protocol: pre‑choose and photo‑document 2–3 micro‑nooks per soft window (cafés/libraries) away from thresholds; never block service counters, queues, muster paths, or egress. Hard cap: max 6 participants visible at once; 10‑minute linger cap; if a crowd forms, hosts disperse and relocate or end the moment.",

🧪 Test Lab
Interactive Agent Dev
🎨 Aesthetic Devising — Phase 1
Load an existing brief, generate a new one, approve, or delete. Run against a campaign slug.

houseplant-botanical-caribbean-2026

Identity Summary

red-team block
Color Palette

#F2F7F4
textOnLight
#1E2B28
Hero Slogan
Sail first, spot the green.

Jewel of the Seas, EN/ES easy hellos.

Voice Persona
Your soft‑spoken plant friend who loves shaded windows and ocean horizons.

Casting Goal
A relaxed mix of plant‑curious travelers: solos, pairs, and small friend duos across ages. Most frames show one to three people in calm cruise moments, with a few family‑friendly scenes that still feel quiet and window‑side. Enforce representation via the casting matrix in Production Bible (see below).

Age Range Guidance
Core 25–60 with believable range: include a few teens (12–17) and several 60+ guests. No kiddie focus and no party‑only energy.

Diversity Intent
Visible diversity in skin tone, ethnicity (including Afro‑Caribbean and Latinx given San Juan embarkation), hair texture, and body type—across the full set, not token shots. Include queer representation and at least one visible mobility aid where feasible without shifting tone.

Pairing Guidance
Vary scene partners: solo at a rail, two friends of different backgrounds, a queer couple at a window, multigenerational duo in a lounge. Rotate who leads the moment; avoid repeating one default pair.

Anti-Stereotype Rules
• No “crazy plant person” tropes or gendered clichés
• No token bilingual ‘host’ as the only Latinx face
• No lab‑coat, expert, or instructor optics
• No leaf headpieces, faux vines, or cosplay
• Do not reduce culture to prints, flags, or palettes
Community Core Promise
Be around your plant community at sea—easy hellos, tiny compliments, and a calmer way to spot your people without turning your vacation into a schedule.

Participation Style
Drop in, drift out, or simply smile and pass by. Lurk‑friendly, low‑pressure, and brief by design—no tracking and no FOMO.

Social Gravity
A shared leaf print, a small enamel pin, or one EN/ES codename quietly signals common ground. Phone galleries with favorite leaf photos spark quick café‑nook chats once seated or at nearby benches—no device handling in active queues.

Copy Framing Rule
Use opt‑in language: “If you feel like it…,” “Swing by for 5–10 minutes,” “Lurk welcome.” Offer soft windows like around breakfast and before dinner—or just say it when you pass by. Never imply reservations, rosters, or exclusive access. Privacy: share your own photos; avoid filming others.

Optional Gatherings
• Morning icon hello with one simple EN/ES hint—sometime in the breakfast window; in and out under 10 minutes
• Early‑evening ‘what you noticed’—before dinner; two or three tiny shares, then dinner
• Sea‑day Camera‑Roll One‑Pick—pop‑by window of 10–12 minutes max; disperse immediately if a crowd forms
• Postcard Peek—bring one flat plant postcard to show‑only, or just listen
Red-Team Summary
Beautifully conceived ambient community cruise concept with excellent ship‑first visuals, strong safety/permissions guardrails, and a robust diversity/casting plan. However, a few compliance and copy consistency issues remain: precise anchor times still appear in campaign metadata, the duration for the sea‑day Camera‑Roll One‑Pick is inconsistent across sections, and ship/operator naming appears in default messaging without an explicit all‑deliverables disclaimer gate. These must be corrected before launch.

block
Approval Recommendation
Block for now. Remove all precise times from metadata and public copy, standardize the sea‑day moment duration, and extend trademark‑disclaimer enforcement to every deliverable (including subSlogan use, video onscreen/description text, and organic social). After these fixes, re‑submit for a quick clearance pass.

Required Fixes
• Remove all precise anchor times (08:30/17:30) from metadata and public copy; replace with colloquial anchors; confirm regex gate scans metadata/CMS fields and passes.
• Resolve the ‘Camera‑Roll One‑Pick’ duration mismatch; use one standard (recommend 10–12 minutes) everywhere, including Production Bible caps.
• Harden trademark‑disclaimer enforcement: either delete ship name from default subSlogan or extend the legal gate to every deliverable (video on‑screen/description, organic social, CMS slogans). Add automated scans that block on non‑compliance.
Issues
blocker
copy alignment
Precise anchor times remain in campaign metadata
Campaign metadata description: “Recognition… repeated at 08:30 and 17:30.” Production Bible also states to block export if any precise time appears in public copy or metadata.

Purge 08:30/17:30 everywhere (metadata and any public units). Replace with soft anchors (“around breakfast,” “before dinner”). Confirm the regex gate scans metadata fields and CMS slugs, then re‑run preflight.

warning
copy alignment
Inconsistent duration for ‘Camera‑Roll One‑Pick’ sea‑day moment
OptionalGatheringMoments lists “One 20‑minute Camera‑Roll One‑Pick,” while CommunityExpression sets a “10–12 minutes max” window and Production Bible sets a 10‑minute linger cap.

Standardize to a single duration (recommend 10–12 minutes) across OptionalGatheringMoments, CommunityExpression, and any scripts. Update caps consistently in the Production Bible.

warning
copy alignment
Ship/operator naming risk in default messaging without universal disclaimer enforcement
Messaging subSlogan: “Jewel of the Seas, EN/ES easy hellos.” Multiple scripts reference “Jewel of the Seas.” Gating currently calls out landing pages, email footers, and paid ads, but not explicitly all deliverables (e.g., organic social, video lower‑thirds/descriptions, CMS subSlogan slots).

Either remove the ship name from default subSlogan and generic placements or extend the trademark‑disclaimer gate to all deliverables (video on‑screen text/descriptions, organic social captions, CMS slogans). Add an automated scan that blocks export if ship/operator appears without the exact disclaimer string.

warning
stereotype risk
Potentially stiff EN/ES micro‑phrases if not localized
Primary phrase “Saludo verde—monstera” may read as formal/unnatural in some Puerto Rican/Caribbean contexts, though a QA note is present.

Finalize EN/ES micro‑phrases after recorded read‑tests with Puerto Rican/Caribbean talent; approve only variants that sound native and gentle. Document final approved phrases in the Producer Packet to prevent drift.

    "globalDirectionNotes": "Vacation first, niche second. Keep the ship and sea leading every frame, with Leaf & Leisure expressed through behavior, spatial choice, or one tasteful cue. Never stage a class or demo. Avoid any non‑native greenery. Maintain small social scale—mostly pairs, occasional serene solo—so the community feels easy and opt‑in. Rotate cues: behavioral nods, shaded seat choice, architectural motifs, one postcard cameo total across a set, one sketchbook, one scarf. Use the single poetic leaf‑shadow only once (S6). Keep storyboards environment‑led so downstream models don’t need human animation; let wake, flags, reflections, and clouds provide motion.\nPermissions, rights, and naming:\n- Ship/operator approvals: Block any onboard capture (handheld or otherwise) without written approval from the cruise line/ship for commercial use. If approvals are not secured, commit deliverables to synthetic/stock‑only assets and label accordingly in exports.\n- Port/terminal approvals: Do not film on piers or terminals without written port/authority clearance. If denied or impractical, default to S7_alt_rail_return.\n- Venue naming/logos: Until written venue approvals are on file, all public‑facing labels remain generic (e.g., “café window seats,” “buffet window,” “atrium,” “forward‑view lounge”). Do not feature venue signage or logos prominently. If approvals are later granted, match labels precisely.\n- Brand/trademark usage: Do not reference “Jewel of the Seas” or “Royal Caribbean International” in any public unit unless contractual permission to name the ship/operator is confirmed. Where referenced, include the exact disclaimer: “Jewel of the Seas and Royal Caribbean International are trademarks of their respective owners and are not sponsors of nor responsible for this program.”\nRelease/preflight checklist (blocker gates):\n- Block export if any branded venue name appears without linked written approval.\n- Block export if any precise time (e.g., 08:30/17:30) appears in public copy or metadata; use colloquial anchors (“around breakfast,” “before dinner”). Apply regex: /\\b([01]?\\d|2[0-3]):[0-5]\\d\\b/.\n- Block export for any onboard footage lacking written ship/operator filming approval.\n- Block export for any pier/terminal footage lacking written port/authority approval.\n- Block export if any shot note contradicts rail posture safety (forearms light, torso upright, hands inside rail line).\n- Block export if any edit depicts device handling in an active queue or filming in/near a line. Add keyword gate: /(queue|line).*(phone|photo|camera|show)/i.\n- Block export if any public unit references the ship/operator without the required trademark disclaimer present (landing pages, email footers, paid ads).\n- Producer must upload a Casting Coverage Tracker mapping every storyboard shot and still (IDs) to on‑camera identities: skin tone/ethnicity, age band, queer visibility, and any mobility aid. Sign‑off required to ensure matrix coverage; block export until complete.\nFallback asset plan: cabin/balcony/window angles (S4, S10 variants), promenade rails (S1, S7_alt_rail_return), library/card room window nooks, and licensed ocean/ship architecture stock without identifiable guests/crew. Maintain ship identity via rail/hull/window architecture; avoid exterior brand marks when licensing. If approvals not secured, deliver synthetic/stock‑only versions and label assets “synthetic/AI‑only” in internal shot lists and exports.\nSafety, privacy, and accessibility guardrails (must‑adhere):\n- No sitting/standing on rails; no leaning beyond the top rail; forearms resting lightly on rail, torso upright; hands remain inside rail line.\n- No device handling in active queues; recognition in/near lines is verbal‑only under 5 seconds; do not linger or film in/near a line—step to adjacent seating/benches for any share.\n- No filming while navigating stairs; keep clear of thresholds, service counters, and active queues.\n- No filming in or near gangways/security operations; port authority instructions override all.\n- Teens on‑camera only with guardian present and documented consent; guardians visible on set for any take.\n- Do not capture identifiable bystanders or crew without written consent; stage frames with clean backgrounds; blur/remove any incidental faces.\n- Phones shown waist‑high, angled down and away from others; no PII (boarding passes, cabin numbers, receipts) in frame.\n- Offer seated options and quiet corners for mobility‑aid users; avoid prolonged standing shots.\n- Include non‑visual participation cues in VO/copy: textures (teak/linen), breeze sounds, ambient ship hums—so low‑vision guests can enjoy the motif without visuals.\n- No drones onboard or in port areas.\nCapture constraints: handheld or compact gimbal only. No cranes, dollies, steadicams, or tripods onboard. Any complex move is AI‑simulated only unless a written ship permit is secured.\nQueue etiquette: any recognition in or near a line is verbal‑only under 5 seconds; no filming; step to adjacent seating for any photo sharing.\nCasting matrix (enforce across shoots; ensure Afro‑Caribbean/Latinx presence in hero units and bilingual delivery):\n- Hero Explainer (video): On‑cam b‑roll only; VO delivered bilingually by Talent A (Afro‑Caribbean woman, 30s) and Talent B (Latinx man, 40s) alternating lines.\n- TikTok Seed: Visible pair includes one Afro‑Caribbean or Afro‑Latina woman and one Asian‑Caribbean or White Latinx non‑male; VO by Talent B (EN) with Talent A (ES tag).\n- Threshold Announcement (VO): Talent A (ES/EN) primary; caption bilingual.\n- Countdown 3‑2‑1: rotate VO among Talent B (EN/ES), Talent C (queer non‑binary Latinx, 20s), Talent D (White Puerto Rican man, 50s).\n- Stills LS1 (hero): Afro‑Caribbean woman + White man (30s–40s).\n- LS2 (hero_alt): Latinx woman + East Asian man (20s–30s).\n- LS3 (concept): Grandparent (Afro‑Caribbean, 60+) + teen (Latinx, 13–16; guardian present).\n- LS4 (email_header): Solo 60+ woman, mid/plus body type.\n- LS5 (social_square): Two women; one uses a slim cane visible at chair side.\n- LS6 (concept, adjacent to queue): Two strangers of different backgrounds (one Latinx, one White/Asian), no filming in line; if captured, staged from adjacent bench.\nQA: Run all EN/ES lines with Puerto Rican/Caribbean talent for naturalness and punctuation; rotate micro‑phrases lightly to avoid chantiness. Include a bilingual copy QA checklist in the producer packet.\nAlt‑asset labeling: Mark any shot that cannot be captured practically as “synthetic/AI‑only” in internal shot lists and exports.\nAnchor micro‑venue and crowd protocol: pre‑choose and photo‑document 2–3 micro‑nooks per soft window (cafés/libraries) away from thresholds; never block service counters, queues, muster paths, or egress.
